meaning: adj.明白的,显明的,率直的adv.全然,完全,彻底
examples: "Thanks" can be useful, as it is able to bridge the divide between the formality of "thank you" and the downright relaxed "cheers".
The man was downright rude to us.
It's not just stupid—it's downright dangerous.
"People were downright giddy to get into a kayak on the Los Angeles River, " he said.
During the go-go stock market of the 1990s, a privately held tech company seemed downright strange.
